description_long: This is a medium-sized crab with a broad, slightly domed carapace that is dark brown on top and lighter orange-brown on the legs and underside. The crab features two prominent claws (chelae), one of which appears slightly larger and lighter in color, and eight walking legs that are jointed and covered in a smooth, hard exoskeleton. The carapace is smooth with subtle textural markings and a glossy finish, while the legs and claws show more matte, slightly rougher surfaces. The eyes are small, dark, and positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, giving the crab a wide field of view. No antennae are clearly visible in these images. The overall appearance is robust and typical of edible crabs found in coastal or urban seafood markets.
description: A robust, dark brown crab with a glossy carapace, two prominent claws, and eight jointed legs, typical of edible crustaceans.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the broad carapace, both claws, and all eight legs spread out symmetrically.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the larger, lighter-colored claw and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the curvature of the carapace and the underside of the legs.
